reviews.review_description:
The videos made the game look decent. 

In the Quest 3, I’m surprised it wasn’t free game. It’s hardly comparable to what was advertised. Cars failed to load. I walked for about half an hour around empty streets & abandoned buildings. Some areas of the ground seem to have invisible blocks in areas where you should be able to move smoothly. 
Shooting things did nothing. 

I bought this on sale for $10 & would very much like a refund. Thanks.

I was eligible for the refund. I had only played it for eight minutes and just got the game today. I didn’t like it that much so I tried to refund it and it said refund not approved, so I tried to look why it didn’t work and it just gave me the refund policy. And I understand the refund policy, but it would not let me refund it, even though I followed the guidelines of the refund policy.
I had 10 minutes on the game and didn’t like it so I tried to refund it and it would not let me. The game is so bad and it is dead. I want a refund.

This game runs and plays terribly.  The map has an extremely limited render distance,  the npc cars do not move smoothly, there's many buildings missing, etc.   It's not just a regular jank vr game.   It's unplayable, buggy,  and a ripoff

There’s no other game like it and that’s why you purchased it so stop hating and wait for the updates to role out. The concept is dope. The game play is dope. I swear most these bad reviews are from literal children that shouldn’t even be playing this game! This game is up and coming with soooo much potential. I can’t wait to see it a year from now. And stop trashing the graphics ITS NOT GTA 5!!!

developer_response.body:
Much love, Optimal_Goose. This is the energy that keeps us building. 🙏
You're right - there's nothing else like this on Quest, and that's exactly what we're going for. We know the game isn't perfect yet, but we're committed to the vision: weekly updates, constant improvement, and listening to players who actually engage with what we're creating.

Appreciate you seeing the potential. More heat drops this week

reviews.review_description: This game has came far since I first found it and gave it a shot. Honestly at first I didn’t like it but upon returning to see its progress I am pleasantly surprised and impressed. Super fun game and I’m excited to see the future of it. Most compare it to gta, and they are not wrong, but I can see this becoming its own thing easily with a little more polish. Huge player base already, very diverse so strap in lol 🤣 and prepare for anarchy 🤌😎

developer_response.body: Hello Lee — we’re sorry to hear your experience was uncomfortable. Refunds are handled directly by Meta under their store policy, so we unfortunately don’t have the ability to issue them ourselves. That said, we genuinely want to help resolve any issues you encountered. Please reach out to us on Discord and we’ll do our best to address your concerns or help improve your experience moving forward.

reviews.review_description: Just a suggestion to the dev’s. Would be cool to be able to choose playing as a cop for RP maps and also having a ticket & arrest system for them as well just to keep it exciting..  almost like a GTA RP type of thing being that they don’t have anything like it on quest yet this game is the closest thing so go all out with it if possible  🙌🙌🙌

developer_response.body: Great suggestion! A cop/roleplay system is definitely on our roadmap. We’re working hard to optimize new features so they run smoothly on all Quest devices, and we’re excited to keep expanding the world of Trap Life VR. Stay tuned—and join the community for updates: http://discord.gg/traplifevr 🙌

The game is really bad it’s basically pay to win you can do anything without having to spend real money in the game it’s clear that they shouldn’t have released it in early access as it’s to buff to do anything

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback, luckysmuggler282.
Just to clarify a few things: Trap Life VR isn't pay-to-win. Every item, vehicle, and weapon can be earned through gameplay - the drug economy, missions, and robberies all generate in-game currency. IAPs are purely optional shortcuts for players who want them.
We're upfront about being in active development, which is why we ship weekly updates based on player feedback. We're building an ambitious multiplayer crime sandbox on mobile VR hardware - that comes with very real technical constraints we're constantly working to optimize.
If the current state doesn't match your expectations, we respect that. We'd love to have you back as the game evolves. Check our Discord for patch notes and upcoming features.

reviews.review_description: Vreatgame

1st off, the dev of this game is the only dev working on it, has some things in his personal life going on, and is still managing to pump out updates and constantly innovating his own work. Been playing about a week, through the updates, and the game just gets better and better. Super excited to see it in 2month or 6 months when its all extremely polished. They said GTA couldnt be done on VR. Well my boy is doing it.

Nice cool gta type open world vr game, the dev does regular updates, online meeting other players, joining gangs, pretty fun gta type online shooter and it's free, so get yo gang grab ya pistols and join the fun
